models.employee: The logged-in employee viewing their dashboard and taking trainings.
models.assignedTraining: A training assigned to the employee with due date, status, and progress information.
models.completedTraining: A training the employee has finished, stored in their archive with score and attempt data.
models.trainingRules: The rules page configuration for a specific training, shown before the employee starts.
models.slide: A single content unit within a training — video, text, or inline question.
models.quizQuestion: A question in the final quiz with answer options and correct answer.
models.attemptResult: The outcome of a completed training attempt including score, pass/fail, and timing.

relationships: Employee has many AssignedTraining and CompletedTraining
AssignedTraining references a Training and its TrainingRules
Each Training has an ordered list of Slides and QuizQuestions
CompletedTraining stores AttemptResult data for each attempt

assignedTrainings.title: Customer Escalation Handling
assignedTrainings.description: How to handle difficult customer situations, de-escalation techniques, and when to involve management.

assignedTrainings.title: Workplace Safety Fundamentals
assignedTrainings.description: Covers essential workplace safety protocols including fire exits, equipment handling, and incident reporting procedures.

assignedTrainings.title: New Hire Onboarding — Q1 2026
assignedTrainings.description: Welcome to the team! This training walks you through company values, benefits, tools, and your first-week checklist.

assignedTrainings.title: Anti-Harassment Policy Training
assignedTrainings.description: Mandatory training covering workplace harassment policies, reporting mechanisms, and bystander intervention strategies.

trainingRules.title: Forklift Safety Certification
trainingRules.description: Covers proper forklift operation, load handling, pedestrian awareness, and emergency procedures required for warehouse floor access.
trainingRules.customInstructions: You must score at least 80% to receive your forklift operating permit. This certification is required before you can operate any powered industrial truck on the warehouse floor. Video segments must be watched in full.

slides.title: Introduction to Forklift Safety
slides.content: Forklifts are essential tools in warehouse operations, but they're also one of the leading causes of workplace injuries. Each year, roughly 85 workers are killed and 34,900 are seriously injured in forklift-related incidents in the United States alone. This training will prepare you to operate a forklift safely and responsibly.

slides.title: Check Your Understanding
slides.content: Before operating a forklift at the start of your shift, what is the first thing you should do?

question.text: Before operating a forklift at the start of your shift, what is the first thing you should do?
options: Start the engine and let it warm up
question.explanation: A thorough pre-operation inspection must be completed before every shift to ensure the forklift is safe to operate.

slides.title: Pedestrian Awareness Zones
slides.content: Warehouse environments mix foot traffic with heavy machinery. Designated pedestrian walkways are marked with yellow floor paint. When operating near intersections, always honk your horn, slow down, and make eye contact with pedestrians before proceeding. Never assume a pedestrian has seen you — always yield.

slides.title: Quick Check
slides.content: When approaching a blind intersection in the warehouse, what should you do?

question.text: When approaching a blind intersection in the warehouse, what should you do?
options: Speed up to clear the intersection quickly
Honk, slow down, and check for pedestrians
Stop and wait for someone to wave you through
question.explanation: Always honk your horn, reduce speed, and visually check for pedestrians before proceeding through any intersection.

slides.title: Tip-Over Prevention
slides.content: Forklift tip-overs account for roughly 25% of all forklift-related fatalities. Key prevention measures: never exceed the rated load capacity, keep loads low and tilted back during travel, avoid sharp turns at speed, and never operate on slopes exceeding the rated grade. If a tip-over occurs, stay in the seat, grip the wheel firmly, brace your feet, and lean away from the direction of the fall. Never attempt to jump from a tipping forklift.

quizQuestions.text: How often should a pre-operation inspection be performed on a forklift?
options: At the start of each shift
Only when a problem is noticed

quizQuestions.text: What is the maximum safe speed for a forklift in a pedestrian area?
What should you do if your forklift begins to tip over?
options: Stay seated, grip the wheel, brace feet, lean away from the fall
Try to counterbalance by shifting your weight

quizQuestions.text: When traveling with a load, how should the forks be positioned?
options: Raised as high as possible for visibility
Lowered 4-6 inches from the floor, tilted slightly back

quizQuestions.text: Who is responsible for ensuring pedestrians are clear before moving a forklift?
What percentage of forklift fatalities are caused by tip-overs?
